original_abstract: This article examines the structural and methodological framework of the Dictionary of Neologisms, a crowdsourcing project hosted by the Language Observatory of the University of Warsaw. By analyzing both its micro- and macro-structure, the author details the criteria for acquiring, selecting, and lexicographically describing new lexical units. The dictionary is contextualized within the broader landscape of traditional and community-driven neography, illustrating how it balances amateur contributions with professional linguistic oversight. Furthermore, the study explores the sociolinguistic factors driving the emergence of contemporary Polish vocabulary—particularly within the decentralized "network society"—while addressing the conceptual ambiguity and temporal challenges inherent in defining what truly constitutes a "neologism."
humanized_abstract: This article focuses on the structure and methodology of the Dictionary of Neologisms created as part of a crowdsourcing project conducted by the Language Observatory at the University of Warsaw. Presented in detail are the criteria determining the addition of lexical items to the dictionary, as well as their ordering within the micro- and macrostructure of the dictionary. The Dictionary is situated in relation to traditional neography and community-based projects in order to demonstrate the balance between amateur and professional elements of amateur lexicography. The article delves into the sociolinguistic factors influencing the creation of contemporary vocabulary of Polish, especially in relation to the “network society” and discusses the problems related to the concept of neologism and to its delimitation in time.
